bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me low utility and shows no concession pattern yet, so I will counter with my strongest values across all issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid while making only a tiny concession to avoid simply repeating my previous offer. The opponent’s two offers are both very low utility for me and their second offer is not an improvement, so there is no evidence yet that they are conceding toward my priorities.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid rather than making meaningful concessions. The opponent’s offers are all valid but remain low-utility for me and do not show clear concession toward my priorities, so I will hold firm on high-weight issues while returning to my strongest package.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid rather than making meaningful concessions. I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still early in the negotiation at t=0.08, so there is little pressure to concede substantially yet. The opponent has moved slightly on issueC/issueD but their offers remain low for my utility, so I will hold firm on my most important issues while varying from my immediately previous bid.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a near-max utility bid while making only a tiny concession on low-weight issueE. I am targeting my maximum-utility bid, since the current time fraction is still very early and there is little pressure to concede. I am targeting a still-strong utility bid while making concessions on lower-weight issues D and E, since it is early but the opponent’s latest offer shows meaningful movement toward my preferences on issueB and issueC. The opponent has made a major concession in the latest offer, especially on my high-priority issueA and issueC, bringing their offer to a reasonable but still early-stage utility for me. Since time is only 0.18, I will not fully accept their low issueB/issueE terms yet, but I can concede on lower-weight issueE and partially on issueD while holding firm on my most important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.82 since it is still early at t=0.20, so I should not concede too much yet. The opponent’s latest offer improved substantially for me on issueA, issueC, and issueD, but they are still pushing very hard on issueB and issueE; I will hold firm on the more important issueB while conceding issueE to their last offered value.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the early-game range while making a modest concession on lower-weight issues to improve acceptability. The opponent has recently conceded on my high-priority issueA and issueC, so I hold those at my best values, while offering flexibility on issueD and issueE where the cost to me is smaller. There are no natural-language messages to rely on, and all observed offers use valid domain values.

I am targeting a utility around 0.75, which is a meaningful concession from my prior offers while still protecting high-weight issueA and issueC. I am targeting a strong but more flexible utility around 0.77: I keep my highest-weight priorities on issueA and issueC, while conceding on issueB and issueE where the opponent has recently signaled interest. Since it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.26, I should not drop too far, but their recent offers have improved substantially for me, especially around issueA/issueB/issueC/issueD, so this counter is close to their latest bid while protecting my key value on issueA.
